page: Amazon Web Services m8gb.12xlarge is a general-purpose server powered by AWS Graviton processors. It features 48 dedicated physical cores on the arm64 architecture, utilizing the AWS ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 model running at 2.8 GHz. The processor includes 64 KB L1d cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 36 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 192.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance runs on the Nitro hypervisor, lacks local storage, and includes a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong multi-core capabilities and exceptional memory throughput. The server achieves top-tier status in large-block memory bandwidth, with read speeds of 435.26 GB/sec and write speeds of 217.09 GB/sec. Multi-threaded operations perform exceptionally well, as evidenced by a top-tier bzip3 decompression speed of 522.44 GB/sec and a high Geekbench multi-core score of 24004.0. However, single-core performance is more moderate, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance and Geekbench text processing falling into the average category. Additionally, while text generation for medium and large LLM models is strong, text generation for small models is poor, highlighting a performance tradeoff depending on workload scale.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the m8gb.12xlarge offers high resource density and strong multi-core performance per resource unit, typical of the Graviton ARMv9 architecture. The absence of bundled local storage and dedicated GPUs makes it a highly specialized option for non-graphical, network-heavy, and memory-bound tasks. It is highly suited for database operations, static web serving, software compilation, and multi-threaded data processing.
description: Amazon Web Services m8gb.12xlarge is an arm64 general-purpose server powered by 48 dedicated physical cores of the AWS ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 processor running at 2.8 GHz. It features 192.0 GB of memory, a 100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, and runs on the Nitro hypervisor, though it lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Benchmarks show top-tier multi-core and memory performance, with large-block memory read bandwidth reaching 435.26 GB/sec and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression at 522.44 GB/sec. Conversely, single-core performance and small-model LLM text generation are average to poor. Qualitatively, the server provides high resource density and excellent multi-core efficiency. It is best suited for database operations, static web serving, software compilation, and multi-threaded data processing.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8gb.12xlarge is an arm64 general-purpose server with 48 dedicated Graviton Neoverse-V2 cores, 192 GB RAM, and 100 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier multi-core performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8gb.12xlarge is an arm64 server with 48 dedicated Graviton cores, 192 GB RAM, and 100 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: An arm64 general-purpose server delivering top-tier memory bandwidth and strong multi-core performance for database and web-serving workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by 48 dedicated AWS ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 physical cores at 2.8 GHz.
Equipped with 192.0 GB of system memory and a 100 Gbps baseline network.
Delivers top-tier large-block memory bandwidth and multi-core decompression speeds.
Lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, relying entirely on network-attached storage.
Optimized for database operations, static web serving, and software compilation.
